resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def canBeTypedWords(self, text: str, brokenLetters: str) -> int:
        broken = set(brokenLetters)
        count = 0
        for word in text.split():
            if not set(word) & broken:
                count += 1
        return count
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: We use a set to store the broken letters for efficient lookups. We then iterate over each word in the text, and for each word, we check if any of its letters are in the set of broken letters. If not, we increment the count of words that can be typed.
